“I live,” Kaelen ne reply kiya. “And I bring warning. The Dusk grows stronger. The shard—” Woh ruk gaya, Elira ki taraf dekha. “The shard has returned.”

Chamber mein ripple gaya. Fear. Awe. Anger.

Elira ka dil dhadak raha tha. Har nazar uski skin ke against knife ki tarah mehsoos ho rahi thi.

Elder ki aankhein tani. “Then this girl carries both hope and doom. Tell me, child—why should we not end you now, before the darkness consumes us all?”

Elira ka muh sukha. Har instinct use piche hatne, ise deny karne, bhagne par majboor kar raha tha. Lekin instead, use apna chin uthaya, apni awaaz ko steady karne par majboor kiya.

“Because I won’t let it consume me. Because I’d rather die fighting for the light than live in shadow.”

Uske words broken chamber mein goonje.

Aur though doubt abhi bhi unki aankhon mein linger kar raha tha, use lagta tha ki woh kuch aur bhi dekh rahi thi.

Hope.

Chapter Nine: Flames of the First Siege

Council chamber ko Elira ki words digest karne ka waqt hi nahi mila jab pehla attack aaya.

Isne distant thunder se shuru kiya—pehle soft, phir growing roar jo cracked walls ko hila raha tha. Corin ki aankhein windows ki taraf snap hui. “Shadowsworn!” usne shout kiya, already apna bow drawing. “They’re here!”

Kaelen ne apni fist table par slam ki. “Everyone outside—now! Elira, stay close!”

Lekin escape karne ka waqt nahi tha. Outer doors ne shudder kiya jab battering ram ne strike kiya, unhe jagged shards mein splinter kar diya. Black-clad figures through pour hui, blades dim light mein glint kar rahi thi. Smoke aur dust ne chamber ko fill kar diya, aankhon ko sting kar raha tha aur saans choke kar raha tha.

Elira ka dil dhadak raha tha. Use apni dagger pakdni chahiye thi—but instinctively, use pata tha ki woh kaafi nahi hogi. Uske pack mein shard throb kar raha tha, jaise uske dar ko sensing, phir se phusphusaa raha tha: Use me. Protect them.

Use sar hilaaya, aage step kiya. Not yet.

Kaelen ne pehli wave ko head-on meet kiya, steel flash hui jab usne swung, sparks ude jab uski blade Shadowsworn ki Dusk-forged swords se clash hui. Tamsin ne flames conjure ki jo sudden suns ki tarah erupt hui, attackers ko corners mein drive kar diya. Corin ki arrows deadly precision se unke marks par pahunchi.

Phir bhi, aur aate hi rahe. Chamber too small tha, enemy too numerous.

Elira ka dimaag race kar raha tha. Use shard ka pulse hotter mehsoos ho raha tha, urging her. One touch, just one. You could end this now.

Uski aankhein frightened council members par gayin, kuch hide karne ki koshish kar rahe the, kuch panic mein frozen. Use allow nahi kar sakti thi ki woh mar jayein—not here, not yet.

Aur phir use ek khayal aaya—a memory of the Guardian’s trial. Your light is yours, not the shard’s. Trust it.

Deep breath ke saath, Elira ne room ke center mein step kiya. Use aankhein band ki, apni chest mein apni light ki warmth mehsoos ki, fragile lekin steady. Use har saans, har heartbeat, har shred of courage par focus kiya jo uske paas bacha tha.

“Leave them!” usne Shadowsworn se kaha, though uski awaaz mein command nahi, sirf intent thi.

Aur phir uske haathon ne pure radiance se flare kiya. Borrowed nahi, stolen nahi—not the shard’s whispering power, but her own.

Light outward surge hui, blinding wave jo smoke aur shadow ko seared. Shadowsworn chillaye, piche stumble huye, unke weapons glow se wash hote hi melt ho gaye. Even Kaelen ko apni aankhein shield karni pari.

Elira ne aankhein kholin. Chamber silent tha. Attackers scattered pade the, kuch unconscious, kuch ruined doors se fleeing. Uske pack mein shard weakly pulse kar raha tha, subdued, jaise sulking ho.

Kaelen uski taraf rush hua, saans ragged. “By the gods… you did it. You actually did it.”

Tamsin ne grin kiya, scorched arm bandaging karte hue wincing. “Well, I’ll be… That’s one way to make an entrance.”

Corin ka bow uske haathon mein slack tha, aankhein wide. “You didn’t touch the shard?”

“No,” Elira ne quietly kaha, trembling. “I… I trusted my own light.”

Kaelen ne disbelief mein sar hilaaya. “Elira, the shard would have been easier. But you did it—your way.”

Council elder, abhi bhi apni cracked marble chair ko pakdi hue, slowly uthi. Uski aankhein awe se chamak rahi thi. “Then perhaps… perhaps there is hope yet. If a child can wield her own light against such darkness, maybe Eryndor can rise again.”

Elira ka pet tight ho gaya. Woh smile karna chahti thi, believe karna chahti thi—but even as words sink huye, use mehsoos hua: shard ki pulse, faint lekin insistent, whispering ki yeh sirf shuruaat hai.

Beyond Eryndor, beyond shattered walls aur fleeing Shadowsworn, Dominion wait kar raha tha. Aur woh use phir test karega.

Lekin abhi, weeks ke baad pehli baar, Elira ne apne aap ko single, quiet breath of relief lene di.

Woh survive kar gaye the.

Chapter Ten: Whispers in the Dark

Council chamber ab quiet tha, attack ki aftermath ne heavy, smoky haze chhod di thi. Council members cautiously move kar rahe the, doors aur windows check kar rahe the, ek doosre se urgent questions whisper kar rahe the. Shadowsworn ke survivors clear ho chuke the, lekin unki presence har kisi ke shoulders par shadow ki tarah linger kar rahi thi.

Elder woman, Lady Seraphine, ne Kaelen aur Elira ko baithne ka ishara kiya. Uska chehra exhaustion aur worry se lined tha, lekin uski aankhein hope ki glimmer le rahi thi.

“You have… done what none of us could,” usne slowly kaha, uski awaaz mein awe aur caution dono. “Light against darkness. Courage where fear would rule. Perhaps… the city may yet endure.”

Elira ki ungliyan uske pack ki strap ko tight kar rahi thi. “We didn’t do it alone. My friends—Kaelen, Tamsin, Corin—they helped.”

Kaelen ne sar hilaaya. “No, Elira. You led. You faced them when the rest of us faltered. That light—it’s yours. We only followed.”

Kisi ke respond karne se pehle hi, room shiver hua. Ek subtle chill hawa mein creep hui, unki skin ko brush kiya. Even hearth ki fire dim si lagne lagi.

Shadows se ek figure aage badhi—cloaked, hood low, face chhupi hui. Har movement deliberate, almost predatory.

“You survived the first siege,” stranger ne kaha, voice smooth, low, aur unnerving. “Impressive. But hope is fragile, little light-bearer.”

Council stiffen ho gayi. Kaelen ka haath apni sword par gaya. Elira instinctively uske saamne step hui.

“Who are you?” Lady Seraphine ne demand kiya, though uski awaaz quaver kar rahi thi.

Stranger ne hood ko itna neeche kiya ki pale, angular features aur dim firelight mein red gleaming eyes reveal hui. Cold, calculating smile uske honton par curve hui.

“I am Avaris,” figure ne kaha. “Some call me the Shadowbroker. Others… the unseen hand. And I am afraid you are all playing my game.”

Tamsin ne apne breath mein mutter kiya, “Well, that escalated quickly.”

Avaris continue kiya, voice calm lekin menace se dripping. “The Dominion is only one force among many. I have watched this city, watched your struggles. The shard in your possession… it is of immense value, not just to you, but to the balance of power. And I intend to claim it, in my own way.”

Elira ka pet tight ho gaya. “You… you want the shard?”

Avaris ki red aankhein glitter hui. “Not want. Need. The shard chooses its bearer, yes—but its power can be guided. Controlled. And I intend to ensure it serves the right hands.”

Kaelen ki thodi tight hui. “You call your hands the right ones? We’ve already faced death once because of the shard. Leave it alone!”

Avaris ne halki si hansi li. “Ah, yeh ziddi rakshak. Taarif ke layak. Par bekar, darne ki baat nahi. Shard ka dhadkan sirf tumhe hi nahi bulata, bacche. Jald hi tum dekhogi ki jo taakatein tumhare khilaaf chal rahi hain, unse ladna itna aasaan nahi. Kuch toh tum pehchaan bhi nahi paogi.”

Usne apna ek phika, gloved haath uthaya, aur kamre ke kinaron ke saaye ghaney ho gaye, dhuen ki tarah council ki taraf raengne lage. Hearth ki aag jaise unke qadmon mein dabti ja rahi thi.

Elira ka dil tez dhadk raha tha. Usne apne pack mein shard ko mehsoos kiya, uska dhadkan ab zyada majboot tha, lagta tha jaise bechain ho raha ho. Lagta tha ki woh Avaris ki maujoodgi ko mehsoos kar raha hai, phir se kuch fusa raha hai — par usne daantien bhaich li, jhukne se inkaar kar diya.

Kaelen aage badha, aur usne Elira ke kandhe par ek surakshit haath rakha. “Chahe tum koi bhi khel khel rahe ho, ajnabi, tum usey chhuoge nahi.”

Avaris muskuraaya, ek curve jo poori tarah thandi dushmani aur mazak dono thi. “Hum dekhenge. Main sabr ka aadmi hoon. Mere paas waqt hai. Aur andhera… hamesha apna raasta banata hai.”

Jab tak koi jawab de pata, Shadowbroker mud gaya, jaise dhuaan ki tarah flickering gloom mein ghul gaya, apne peeche sirf khamoshi chhod gaya.

Council thar-tharaya. Lady Seraphine ke haath kaanp rahe the jab woh unhe fold kar rahi thi. “Humare… Shadowsworn se pare dushman hain. Woh khatre jo humne soche bhi nahi the. Aur phir bhi… tum zinda bachi ho, bacche. Shayad tum hi woh chaabi ho jo un sab ke khilaaf khadi ho sakti ho.”

Elira ne mushkil se gale se hawa nikali, uske vichaar bhatak rahe the. Shard halka sa dhadak raha tha, phir se fusa raha tha — lekin iss baar, taakat ke liye nahi. Sirf ehtiyaat ke liye.

Kaelen ki nazar uski aankhon se mili, sthir, be-dol. “Jo bhi agla ho, hum usey saath mein face karenge. Tum isme akeli nahi ho.”

Elira ne sar hila diya. Woh is baat par yaqeen karna chahti thi, aur hafte baad pehli baar, usne umeed karne ki himmat ki. Lekin uske seenay ke andar, woh ehsaas hata nahi pa rahi thi ki asli imtihaan abhi shuru hua hai.

Bahar, Eryndor khamoshi mein intezaar kar raha tha. Aur kahin, anjaan, Avaris dekh raha tha.

Chapter Eleven: Webs of Deception

Subah ki roshni council chamber ke crack hue windows se kamzor si chhan rahi thi. Hawa mein dhool latki hui thi, aur ek waqt ka grand hall ab dhuen aur geeli pathar ki boo aata tha. Elira khamoshi se baithi thi, apna pack apne paas, sun rahi thi jab Lady Seraphine situation samjha rahi thi.

“City fragile hai,” budhi aurat ne kaha, awaaz chinta se tight. “Tumhari roshni ka display hone ke baad bhi, yahan kayi log shard se dartey hain — aur jo nahi darte… woh dartey hain ki woh kya kheench leta hai.”

Kaelen ek tooti hui pillar ke sahare khada tha. “Shadowsworn kabhi haath nahi kheenchenge. Woh phir se strike karenge, shayad jald hi. Aur ab, Avaris hai — yeh Shadowbroker. Hum nahi jaante uska poora plan, lekin woh clearly city ke andar logon ko manipulate kar raha hai.”

Elira ki ungliyan apne dagger ke kinare trace kar rahi thi. Shard abhi khamosh tha, halka sa dhadak raha tha, lagta tha jaise bechain ho. “Hum yahan baithe nahi reh sakte,” usne kaha. “Agar Dominion — ya Avaris — strike karta hai jab hum intezaar kar rahe hain, toh city gir jayegi pehle hi jab hum ladne ki koshish bhi na kar paayein.”

Tamsin ki aankhon mein aag chamki, dono haathon mein aur chehre pe bhi. “Toh hum apne rules banayenge. Koi bhi humein shadows se manipulate nahi karne wala.”

Corin, jo hamesha scout raha, khamoshi se sun raha tha. “Hum intelligence chahiye,” usne kaha. “Agar Avaris ke log Eryndor ke andar hain, toh humein pata hona chahiye ki woh kaun hain, pehle hi jab woh city ko humare khilaaf mod den.”

Lady Seraphine ne dheere se sar hilaaya. “Tum sahi ho. Hamesha whispers hote hain… lekin naam chhupaye hote hain, dar ke neeche. Agar Avaris ko shard chahiye, toh woh un logon ko modne ki koshish karega jo usey maante hain — ya woh desperate log jo usey maan lenge.”

Elira ko thandak mehsoos hui. Woh clearly dekh sakti thi: city khud ek chessboard ki tarah thi, aur woh abhi seekh rahi thi ki kaun se pieces par bharosa kiya ja sakta hai.

Kaelen ne awaaz kam, lekin firm rakhte hue kaha. “Hum alag ho jayenge. Tamsin aur main northern walls ki taraf threat ke liye scout karenge. Corin, tum marketplaces check karo. Elira… council ke saath raho. Unhe tumhari guidance chahiye hogi. Tumhari roshni unhe inspire kar sakti hai, dikhana ki woh helpless nahi hain.”

Elira ne sar hila diya, lekin uske pet mein bechaini thi. “Agar Avaris seedha mujhe target kare?”

Kaelen ka haath uske kandhe par pada. “Toh hum ready honge. Jo bhi woh humpe phenke, hum usey saath mein face karenge.”

Din guzarta gaya, Elira council members aur citizens ke beech ghoomti rahi, guidance aur tasalli deti rahi. Whisper fail rahi thi ki ladki jo Shadowsworn siege survive kar gayi, aur dheere-dheere, city ne usey ek threat nahi, balki ek beacon samajhna shuru kar diya.

Lekin, jaise hi umeed ki lau jalti, shadows anjaan taur pe move kar rahe the. Jab woh guzarti, doors chupke se band hoti. Crowd mein chehre pal bhar ke liye uski taraf suspicious nazar se dekhte, aankhen patli karke, phir door dekhne lagte. Avaris ki subtle influence kaam kar rahi thi, doubt ko zehron ki tarah darogon ke dilon mein bhar rahi thi.

Shaam ko, Corin sabse pehle wapas aaya, uski brows tight thi. “Northern walls abhi ke liye shaant hain. Kuch bada nahi, lekin… signs hain. Kisi ne hamare patrols ko map kiya hai. Avaris sirf dekh raha nahi hai. Woh plan kar raha hai.”

Tamsin kuch der baad wapas aayi, uske baal jhulse hue, gaalon par kaali mitti lagi thi. “Patrols ne eastern gate ke paas movement dekha. Kuch aisa nahi jo hum pakad paate, lekin jo bhi woh kar raha hai… systematic hai. Woh strings kheench raha hai.”

Kaelen ki aankhen patli hui. “Woh city ko andar se fragile bana raha hai. Trust ko divide kar raha hai, fear faila raha hai. Yehi uska weapon hai.”

Elira baithi, sun rahi thi, dil tez dhadak raha tha. Shard phir se halka sa dhadak raha tha, lekin usne usey chhuna mana kiya. Iski jagah, usne apni khud ki roshni se calm aur determination summon ki. Woh khadi hui aur un council members se bola jo shaam ki session ke liye ikatthe hue the.

“Tum pehle bhi attacks survive kar chuke ho,” usne awaaz sthir rakhte hue kaha, “aur tum phir se survive karoge. Avaris anjaan ho sakta hai, lekin hum uske strike se pehle act kar sakte hain. Hum dar se zyada majboot hain, shadow se zyada majboot, aur us andhere se zyada majboot jo is city ko modne ki koshish kar raha hai.”

Chamber mein assent ki goonj faili. Pehli baar, usne genuine umeed mehsoos ki, chahe woh kitni hi fragile kyun na ho.

Lekin jab suraj horizon ke neeche dooba, danger ki halki fusaar baaki thi. Bahar, hawa nayi thand le kar aayi. City ke galliyon aur chhaton ke beech kahin, Avaris ki aankhen dekh rahi thi. Aur uske game ke pieces move ho rahe the.

Elira ne us weight ko apne seenay mein settle hone diya: Dominion sabr ka hai. Avaris sabr ka hai. Aur woh… usne abhi shuru kiya tha samajhna ki shard — aur apni khud ki roshni — ko wield karna sachmuch kya matlab rakhta hai.

Raat lambi thi, whispers, dar, aur aane waale imtihaanon ke vaade se bhari hui.

Chapter Twelve: Flames of Preparation

Subah Eryndor par ek phiki, bechain roshni ke saath uthti. Elira jaldi uthi, cracked streets par Kaelen, Tamsin, aur Corin ke saath nikli. Chahe city fitfully so rahi thi, woh hawa mein tension mehsoos kar sakti thi: whispered conversations, doors cautiously opening, skyline ki taraf darting eyes.

“Humare paas zyada waqt nahi hai,” Kaelen ne move karte hue kaha. “Agar Avaris strike karta hai jab city unprepared hai, toh tumhari roshni bhi isko bacha nahi paayegi.”

Elira ne sar hila diya, ungliyan apne pack ke strap ko chhu rahi thi. Shard khamosh tha, lagta tha jaise sabr kar raha ho. Usne usey chhuna mana kiya. Iss baar, woh apni khud ki strength par rely karegi.

Tamsin ne apni ungliyon ko crack kiya. “Achha. Kyunki last thing jo humein chahiye, woh hai koi mysterious manipulator jo tumhe power ka temptation de raha ho jab log mar rahe hain.”

Corin ne grin kiya, streets scan karte hue. “Dekhte hain kaun loyal hai — aur kaun already turn ho chuka hai. Mere har corner mein eyes hain.”

Council chamber unka command center ban gaya. Elira, Kaelen, aur Tamsin ne citizens ke chhote groups ko train kiya — basic combat, fire magic control, defensive formations. Elira ne unhe sirf tactics nahi, inspiration se guide kiya. Usne Shadowsworn siege ki stories share ki, ki ordinary logon ne kaise fight back kiya aur survive kiya, strength ke upar courage ko emphasize kiya.

“Tumhe magic ki zaroorat nahi hai,” usne crude swords pakde young men aur women ke group se kaha, “darkness mein light banne ke liye. Tumhari himmat, tumhara resolve, tumhare choices — wohi asli taakat hain.”

Kuch sunte, hesitant, aankhen dar se wide. Dusre, jaise ek lanky ladka Miren, ne nayi himmat payi. Dopahar tak, city streets hum karne lagi — dar se nahi, balki preparation ki cautious umeed se.

Lekin shadows hamesha linger karte the.

Shaam ko, jab Elira eastern gate ke paas training oversee kar rahi thi, alleyways se ek figure emerge hui. Cloaked aur khamosh, shadow darkness mein ghul gaya, aankhen jaise glowing embers. Ek note uske pairon ke paas dhool mein slide hua:

"Tumhari roshni bright hai, lekin brightness andha karti hai. Trust carefully, bacche, warna sab kuch kho dogi."

Elira ka pet tight ho gaya. Avaris. Uska manipulation precise tha. Woh abhi attack nahi kar raha tha — lekin usey test kar raha tha, uske decisions probe kar raha tha, trust ko undermine kar raha tha bina khud ko reveal kiye.

Kaelen ne jhuk kar kaha. “Tumhare shadow wale dost ka aur message?”

Elira ne note ko tight pakda, sar hilaaya. “Yeh ek warning hai… ya threat. Woh mujhe doubt karwana chahta hai. Humein ek doosre ke khilaaf modna chahta hai.”

Tamsin ki aag uske haathon mein flicker kar rahi thi. “Toh usey try karne do. Humne Shadowsworn face kiye hain. Hum survive kar chuke hain. Hum usey bhi survive karenge.”

Corin, jo usually sabse carefree rehta tha, frowned. “Humhe ek plan chahiye. Agar woh chahta hai ki hum fail ho… toh hum usey satisfaction nahi denge.”

Raat giri, apne saath uneasy khamoshi le kar aayi. Elira ramparts par khadi thi, city ko dekh rahi thi. Usne apne pack mein shard ko mehsoos kiya, halka lekin insistent, jaise tension ko mehsoos kar raha ho. Uske whispers tempting the — strength, power, victory ka vaada — lekin usne apni jaib bhaich li.

Nahi. Abhi nahi.

“Yeh city nahi giregi,” usne khud se kaha, apni khud ki roshni ki garmi se draw karte hue. “Jab tak mere paas saans hai. Jab tak hum saath khade hain.”

Uske peeche, city ke defenders weapons sharpen kar rahe the aur prayers whisper kar rahe the. Kahin, Avaris dekh raha tha. Kahin, Dominion intezaar kar raha tha.

Aur Elira jaanti thi, heartbeat ki quiet certainty ke saath, ki agla imtihaan pichle se kai guna zyada khatarnak hoga.

Lekin woh ready hogi.

Chapter Thirteen: The Shadow’s Trap

Agli subah, Eryndor bechaini se jagi. Guards tighter formations mein patrol kar rahe the, aur usually carefree streets bhi tense lag rahi thi. Elira Kaelen aur Tamsin ke saath city mein ghoom rahi thi, un defenders ko check kar rahi thi jinhone usse train kiya tha. Har chehra cautious hope reflect kar raha tha — lekin dar bhi.

Corin uske paas breathless aa pahuncha. “Humhe trouble hai. Eastern gate ke paas kuch off hai. Civilians ne strange figures report kiye hain, silently shadows ke beech move kar rahe hain.”

Elira ka pet tight ho gaya. Avaris. Usne apna pack pakda, shard ki faint, insistent pulse ko resist kiya. Woh phir se fusa raha tha: Bas ek touch, aur tum ise ek pal mein khatam kar dogi.

Nahi. Abhi nahi.

Kaelen ki aankhen dark hui. “Avaris humein test kar raha hai. Jo bhi ho raha hai, woh deliberate hai. Woh chahta hai ki tum apne choices par doubt karo.”

Woh eastern gate ki taraf cautiously approach kiye. Shadows unnatural taur pe lambey the, morning sun mein twist kar rahe the. Black cloaked figures rooftops aur alleyways ke saath slip kar rahe the, group ke paas pahunchne se pehle hi disappear ho ja rahe the. City ke defenders tense the, apne weapons tight pakde, lekin woh Elira par bharosa kar rahe the, usse guidance ke liye dekh rahe the.

Achanak, gate ke neeche ki zameen hil gayi. Ek gehri, unnatural rumble streets mein echo hui. Defenders chillaye, swords aur shields nikale.

Aur phir trap reveal hua.

Gate ke portions inward collapse hue, heavy stone slabs neeche gir gaye. Rubble se figures emerge hui: Shadowsworn, eerie constructs of shadow aur smoke se reinforced, unnaturally shifting forms. Unmein se ek figure zyada lamba khada tha, deep black cloak mein, aankhen molten red — khud Avaris.

“Welcome to the true test, child of light,” usne smoothly kaha. “Chalo dekhte hain ki tumhare paas sachmuch kitni himmat hai.”

Elira ki pulse tez hui. Usne shard ko thrum mehsoos kiya jaise impatient ho, chaos ki taraf kheench raha ho. Usey. Ise khatam karo.

Usne daantien bhaich li aur sar hila diya. “Nahi. Mujhe tumhari zaroorat nahi hai.”

Kaelen ne apna sword nikala, Tamsin ne dono haathon mein flames ignite ki, aur Corin ne apne bow par arrows notch kiye. “Stand firm,” Kaelen ne command di. “Hum inhe yahan rokenge. Civilians ki suraksha karo.”

Elira akele aage badhi. Shard faintly throb kar raha tha, jaise uski defiance ko mehsoos kar raha ho, lekin usne aankhen band kar li aur apni inner light par draw kiya. Uske haath glow karne lage, warmth radiating hui, dust-choked street ko illuminate karti hui.

Shadowsworn piche hat gaye, brightness se hiss karte hue, lekin Avaris ne haath uthaya. Shadows waves ki tarah shift hue, darkness ki barriers banate hue apni forces ko shield karne ke liye.

“Tum apni roshni se jeet nahi sakte,” usne hissa, awaaz unnaturally echo karti hui. “Temptation tumhe guide karegi. Shard tumhe majboot banayega. Kyun resist kar rahi ho?”

Elira ki ungliyan uske side ke dagger ko chhu rahi thi. Usne shard ko stronger pulse mehsoos kiya. Power uske dimaag mein fusa raha tha, effortless victory ka vaada. Lekin usne firm sar hila diya. “Kyunki main apni khud ki strength choose karti hoon. Main himmat choose karti hoon, andhera nahi.”

Ek cheekh ke saath, usne apne haath aage badha diye. Light ne ek blinding wave mein erupt kiya, shadows se takraaya. Street hil gayi, darkness ke constructs chillaye jab woh unravel hone lage. Avaris peeche hat gaya, aankhen patli hui, clearly impressed — lekin defeated nahi.

City ke defenders rally hue, uske display se inspired. Kaelen aur Tamsin aage badhe, remaining Shadowsworn ko kaat kar. Corin ki arrows deadly precision se unke marks par pahunchi.

Aur phir bhi, jab immediate threat gira, Elira jaanti thi ki yeh khatam nahi hua tha. Avaris abhi ke liye peeche hat gaya tha — lekin uska plan, uska trap, uska manipulation, city ko shake kar chuka tha. Fear smoke ki tarah linger kar raha tha, aur shard ki fusaar abhi bhi thi, subtle lekin present, power ka vaada agar woh yield kar de.

Elira ne apni mutthi bhaich li, inner light se strength draw karti hui. “Main usey control nahi hone doongi,” usne kaha. “Abhi nahi, kabhi nahi.”

Lekin uske seenay ke andar, ek cold certainty settle hui. Shadowbroker ne abhi apne games shuru kiye the. Aur uski himmat, uski loyalty, aur uski roshni ke asli imtihaan abhi baaki the.

Chapter Fourteen: The Hidden Ally

Eryndor ki city cautious recovery mein simmer kar rahi thi. Streets jo pehle dar se khamosh thi, ab hammers ki chhan-chhan, boots ki shuffle, aur citizens ki whispered conversations se halki si buzz kar rahi thi. Elira unke beech move kar rahi thi, uski roshni ek quiet guide, confidence aur himmat instilling kar rahi thi, lekin Avaris ki manipulations ki chhaon har kone par thi.

Kaelen, Tamsin, aur Corin council chamber mein usse mile, unke chehre grim. “Hum uske agle strike ka intezaar nahi kar sakte,” Kaelen ne kaha. “Humhe information chahiye. Humhe samajhna hoga ki woh city ke andar kisko influence kar raha hai.”

Elira ne sar hila diya. “Toh hum streets ki khoj karenge. Logon se baat karenge. Pata karenge ki kaun trustworthy hai.”

Tamsin ki aag uske haathon mein faintly glimmer kar rahi thi. “Aur tumhe lagta hai kaun itna brave hoga jo Shadowbroker se darte hue humari madad karega?”

Corin smirk kiya. “Kahin bahar, koi aisa hai jo woh baatein jaanta hai jo Avaris bhi expect nahi karta. Humhe bas ek spark chahiye.”

Ghante beet gaye. Woh alleyways, markets, aur abandoned halls mein quietly move karte rahe. Kuch citizens ne useful intelligence di; dusre dar ke peeche chhup gaye. Strange meetings, sudden disappearances, aur shadowed figures ki whispers ne unhe eventually lower districts ki taraf le gaya, jahan Eryndor ke sabse garib rehte the.

Ek narrow courtyard unke saamne khuli, uski deewarein ivy se coat hui thi. Uske center mein, ek solitary figure intezaar kar rahi thi — ek young woman muted gray cloak mein, uski aankhen alert aur wary.

“Tum Shadowbroker ki khoj kar rahe ho?” aurat ne poochha unke bolne se pehle hi.

Elira ne Kaelen se glance exchange kiya. “Haan. Aur tum…?”

Aurat ne apna hood neeche kiya, ek chehra reveal kiya jo dark, tightly braided hair se frame tha. Uski aankhen intelligence aur resolve se chamak rahi thi. “Main Selara hoon. Maine city ko kaafi dekha hai ki uske patterns jaanoon. Main unhe gather kar rahi hoon jinhon woh sway nahi kar sakta, jo sacchi loyalty ke saath ladenge.”

Kaelen ka haath apne sword par gaya, lekin Elira ne haath uthaya. “Kya hum usey trust kar sakte hain?”

Selara ne faintly muskuraaya. “Agar trust earn ki jaati hai, toh haan. Lekin yeh jaano: maine dekha hai ki jab fear courage ko overtake karta hai. Avaris ise achhe se usey karta hai.”

Tamsin ne murmur kiya, “Finally, koi useful.”

Selara aage badhi, ek small, intricate map nikali. “Yahan. Gates reinforced hain, haan, lekin city ke andar corridors hain — hidden paths, passages, purane tunnels — jo woh monitor nahi kar sakta. Hum inhe usey kar sakte hain intelligence gather karne ke liye, swiftly strike karne ke liye, aur civilians ko protect karne ke liye pehle hi jab woh unhe further manipulate kare.”

Elira ne map ko study kiya, uska pulse tez hua. Yeh woh edge tha jo unhe chahiye tha. Selara ki knowledge aur uski khud ki roshni se courage inspire karne ke saath, woh finally Avaris ki manipulations ke khilaaf fight back kar sakte the jo Eryndor ko andar se unravel karne ki koshish kar rahi thi.

Kaelen ne sar hila diya. “Toh hum dusk par move karenge. Hum apne allies gather karenge, compromised citizens identify karenge, aur second strike ke liye prepare karenge. Humhe nahi hone dena ki fear city ki fate dictate kare.”

Selara ki aankhen glimmer hui. “Aur tum, child of light… tumhari himmat example set karegi. Woh tumhare peeche ayenge, lekin sirf agar tum steadfast raho. Avaris woh test karega. Woh hamesha power se pehle resolve test karta hai.”

Elira ne gale se hawa nikali, apne pack mein shard ka weight mehsoos kiya, subtle lekin present. Usne usey ignore kiya, apne aas-paas ke logon par focus kiya, un defenders par jo usne train kiye the, aur citizens mein blossom kar rahi quiet umeed par.

“Hum ready hain,” usne firmly kaha. “Jo bhi woh humpe phenke, hum usey saath mein face karenge. Koi — na shadow, na fear — is city ko humse nahi chheen sakta.”

Bahar, city ne saans rok li. Kahin andhere mein, Avaris dekh raha tha, plotting, waiting. Lekin pehli baar, Elira jaanti thi ki unke paas sirf himmat nahi — strategy, allies, aur ladne ki ichha bhi thi.

Aur yeh kuch aisa tha jo Shadowbroker bhi immediately manipulate nahi kar sakta tha.

Chapter Twenty: The Choice of Light

Final confrontation ka chamber Elira ne pehle kabhi nahi dekha tha. Black stone ke towering pillars floor se uth rahe the, ancient runes engraved, jo dark energy se pulse kar rahe the. Shadows serpents ki tarah coil kar rahe the, har kadam ke saath ghaney ho rahe. Door ke end par, Avaris intezaar kar raha tha, uski presence ne darkness ko command kiya hua tha, uski red eyes uspar fixed thi jaise molten fire.

Elira ke haath faintly glow kar rahe the — shard se nahi, balki us courage aur roshni se jo woh apne andar carry karti thi. Uske aas-paas, Kaelen, Tamsin, Corin, aur Selara ready khade the, lekin woh jaanti thi ki yeh battle uska tha — sirf Avaris ke khilaaf nahi, balki shard ki temptation ke khilaaf bhi.

Hawa heavy thi, electric. Avaris ki awaaz chamber mein echo hui: “Tum majboot ho gayi ho, child of light. Lekin tumhe dekhna hi padega — shard hi key hai. Usey lo, aur tum ise ek pal mein khatam kar dogi. Inkaar karo, aur sab kuch jo tum pyaar karti ho, jal jayega.”

Elira ne shard ko violently pulse mehsoos kiya apne pack mein, jaise zinda ho, power ka vaada fusa raha ho, usey act karne ke liye urge kar raha ho. Usne sar hila diya, gehri saans li. “Nahi,” usne kaha, “meri choices meri khud ki hain. Main shortcuts ke temptation se nahi jhukungi.”

Ek sudden motion ke saath, Avaris ne shadows aage bheje, monstrous forms mein twist kar diye, defenders ko crush karne ki koshish kar raha tha. Kaelen aur Tamsin turant action mein aaye, steel aur fire ne shadows se head-on takkar li. Corin ki arrows hawa mein streak hui, deadly accuracy se strike karti hui, jabki Selara ne hidden passages se unhe advantage dene mein guide kiya.

Elira firm khadi rahi, uski roshni outward expand hui. Shard phir se pulse kiya, insistent, almost demanding her attention. Lekin usne focus kiya, apne andar ki warmth par — courage, hope, aur apne companions ki unity.

Avaris ne garaj kar kaha, aage badhte hue. “Tum unstoppable ho sakti thi! Ek touch, aur tum sab kuch khatam kar deti. Khud se kyun inkar kar rahi ho?”

Elira ne apne haath uthaye, radiance storm clouds ke beech se nikalti suraj ki tarah flare hui. “Kyunki asli taakat aasaan raaste lena nahi hai! Yeh sahi cheez choose karna hai, chahe woh kitna bhi mushkil kyun na ho. Main nahi hone doongi ki fear ya temptation mujhe guide kare. Meri roshni… meri khud ki hai!”

Chamber hil gaya jab uski roshni ne darkness se takraaya. Shadowsworn aur constructs shatter hue, nothingness mein chillate hue. Avaris lurch kara, sirf apni mastery of shadows se shield hua, lekin even woh Elira ki unwavering resolve ki brilliance ke saamne nahi tik sakta tha.

Woh uske paas aage badhi, shard gently pulse kar raha tha, ab demanding nahi, sirf acknowledging. Uske dimaag mein usne path dekha — shard ka asli purpose — na weapon blindly wield karne ke liye, balki balance restore karne ka guide.

Avaris ne apna haath uthaya, shadows final assault ke liye coil hue, lekin Elira ki roshni ne brighter flare kiya, usse warmth aur clarity mein envelop kar liya. “Yeh khatam hota hai, Avaris. Power se nahi, fear se nahi… balki choice se. Himmat se. Unity se.”

Ek lambi, tense pal tak, usne resist kiya, phir lurch kara. Uske aas-paas ke shadows dissolve hone lage, uski conviction ke force ke neeche. Finally, woh collapse kar gaya, uski red eyes dim hui, sirf ek whisper chhoda: “Yeh… khatam nahi hua…” phir nothingness mein vanish ho gaya.

Chamber khamosh ho gaya. Elira ki roshni gentle glow mein settle hui, uska jism exhaustion se kaanp raha tha. Uske companions uske paas daude, unke chehron par awe aur relief.

Selara aage badhi, uske kandhe par haath rakha. “Tumne kar dikhaya. Tumne wisely choose kiya. Shard ki power acknowledge hui, lekin misuse nahi hui. City… aur balance… safe hain.”

Kaelen muskuraaya, apna sword sheath kar liya. “Humne tumhari roshni follow ki, Elira. Kyunki tumne temptation nahi, himmat choose ki.”

Tamsin ki flames softly flicker hui, warmth Elira ki glow ke saath harmony mein radiating. “Aur yeh woh roshni hai jo koi andhera overcome nahi kar sakta.”

Elira ne apne pack mein shard ko dekha. Woh faintly pulse kar raha tha, jaise heartbeat, lekin ab insistent nahi, demanding nahi. Usne faintly muskuraaya, finally samajh kar: shard blindly wield karne ke liye nahi tha. Woh ek guide tha, ek teacher, aur ek test of one’s heart.

Bahar, Eryndor phir se saans lene laga, city abhi ke liye safe. Citizens apne gharon se cautiously emerge hue, streets mein umeed ki whispers fail rahi thi. Shadowsworn gayab, Avaris defeated — ya kam se kam rok diya gaya — aur aage ka raasta illuminated tha, na magic se alone, balki himmat, choice, aur unity se.

Elira ne exhale kiya, apni khud ki roshni ki garmi se apna seena bharta hua. “Humne kar dikhaya,” usne kaha. “Saath mein, humne kar dikhaya.”

Aur kahin, city se pare, ek nayi subah uthi, peace ki taraf ishara karti hui — lekin yeh bhi warning deti hui ki duniya hamesha himmat, choices, aur temptation resist karne ki strength maangti rahegi.

Elira ne faintly muskuraaya, apne companions ki taraf mudti hui. “Jo bhi agla ho… hum usey ek ho kar face karenge.”
Aur pehli baar, shard ne gently pulse kiya — lure ki tarah nahi, balki ek quiet guide ki tarah, heart ki victory ko acknowledge karte hue temptation se, aur roshni ki victory ko andhere se.
